ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-17-2024 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2024 Continued To Apr-30-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Jan-05-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for Default judgment prove up hearing as to Defendant Xiaoqui Wu.

Dept:505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Bailiff: Deputy Bastain; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:44:35 a.m. and concluding at 10:02:28 a.m.(D505)

Plaintiff Guohui Zhang appeared in pro per, was placed under oath and testified.
Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu not appearing.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Defamation filed on November 18, 2019.
Proof of personal service as to Defendant filed November 20, 2019
Default entered as to Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu filed December 23, 2019.
Notice of order denying Defendant's motion to set-aside the entry of default filed November 20, 2023.
Plaintiff to summarize and address the causes of action.
Court reviews the statements submitted.
Court confirms with the Plaintiff that all the allegations and statements are true and correct.

Based on the testimony and statements made, Plaintiff has proved his prima facie case and Court finds in favor of the Plaintiff Guohui Zhang and against Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu as follows:

1. Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu must delete all his published wrongful statements about and against Plaintiff Guohui Zhang on the internet.

2. Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu shall not publish and/or disseminate any derogatory, defamatory or disparaging statements pertaining to Plaintiff Guohui Zhang on any social media platform(s).

3. Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu must delete and return Plaintiff Guohui Zhang's confidential information including the social media platform "WeChat" account information of Plaintiff Guohui Zhang's clients to Plaintiff Guohui Zhang.

4. Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu shall not publish defaming videos and statements of Plaintiff Guohui Zhang in any/all online presence on the internet.

5. Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu shall not use any of Plaintiff Guohui Zhang's confidential information for any purpose.

6. Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu shall not contact any of Plaintiff Guohui Zhang's active clients to further harass, slander and libel Plaintiff Guohui Zhang.

7. Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu is ordered to delete all the published defaming videos and statements of and towards Plaintiff Guohui Zhang published by Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu in any/all online presence on the internet.

It is so ordered.

Plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order for review and signature by the Court.

Off Calendar Apr-03-2020 Continued To Jul-22-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Set Aside The Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Feb-21-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Xiaoqiu Wu, T:626.586.5978, defendant/ moving party, appearing on his own behalf;
Guohui Zhang, T:415.813.4374, plaintiff/ opposing party, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: Defendant contests/argues the tentative ruling. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT XIAOQIU WU's Motion To Set Aside The Entry Of Default as follows:

Defendant Xiaoqiu Wu's motion to set aside entry of default is denied. In his one-page motion, which lacks any supporting declaration or memorandum of points and authorities, Defendant does not deny that he was personally served with the summons and complaint in this action on November 20, 2019, as reflected in the proof of service of summons. The court notes, however, that the address at which Defendant was personally served differs from the address on plaintiff's proof of service. Nevertheless, it appears that Defendant was aware of the pendency of this litigation, since plaintiff Guohui Zhang certified in writing under penalty of perjury that on November 25, 2019, he notified Defendant that he would be seeking a TRO and OSC by emailing Defendant, sending him a text message, and sending a message to Defendant's Wechat account. Defendant does not make any showing of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect within the meaning of Code of Civ. Proc. 473(b) that would warrant setting aside the default.

In addition, the Court modified its ruling and denied the motion without prejudice to defendant refiling it upon a proper showing under penalty of perjury.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
